creator: cjc@freebsd.org
text:
On Sat, Jan 19, 2002 at 03:53:00AM -0800, sheldonh@FreeBSD.ORG wrote:
> Synopsis: new options for pppd
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: closed->open
> State-Changed-By: sheldonh
> State-Changed-When: Sat Jan 19 03:51:12 PST 2002
> State-Changed-Why: 
> The originator drew my attention to this PR after it timed out in
> feedback state.
> 
> Looking at it, I'm not sure why it was never handed over to the
> userland PPP maintainer.

'Cause ppp(8) and pppd(8) are completely separate beasts?

  $ fgrep -r MAINTAINER /usr/src/usr.sbin/pppd
  /usr/src/usr.sbin/pppd/Makefile:MAINTAINER=  peter@freebsd.org

And it has a different maintainer? (Not that there has been much
maintaining for a long time since it is mostly depricated in favor of
ppp(8)).
-- 
Crist J. Clark                     |     cjclark@alum.mit.edu
                                   |     cjclark@jhu.edu
http://people.freebsd.org/~cjc/    |     cjc@freebsd.org
